definition: The process of receiving or giving systematic instruction, especially at a school or university
example: One child, one teacher, one book, one pen can change the world. Education is the only solution.

definition: The holding of extreme political or religious views; fanaticism
example: The extremists are afraid of books and pens. The power of education frightens them.

definition: Combative and aggressive in support of a political or social cause
example: The militant groups sought to silence the voices of girls.

definition: Relating to the Nobel Prize, awarded annually for outstanding contributions
example: She became the youngest Nobel laureate in history.

definition: A person who is honored with an award for outstanding creative or intellectual achievement
example: As a Nobel laureate, she spoke for millions of children denied education.

definition: An instrument for writing or drawing with ink
example: The extremists are afraid of books and pens. The power of education frightens them.

definition: Not deterred by danger or pain; brave
example: The courageous girl defied the Taliban to attend school.

definition: Open resistance to authority or opposition
example: Her defiance inspired millions around the world.

definition: Complete absence of sound; the fact or state of abstaining from speech
example: We realize the importance of our voices only when we are silenced.

definition: A river valley and district in Pakistan where Malala grew up
example: In the valley of Swat, girls dared to dream of education.

definition: A regularly updated website or web page, typically run by an individual or small group
example: She wrote a blog for the BBC about life under Taliban rule.

definition: The murder of an important person for political or religious reasons
example: The attempted assassination could not silence her voice.

definition: A return to a normal state of health, mind, or strength
example: Her recovery was watched by the entire world.

definition: Her own name as a symbol of resistance
example: I am Malala. My world has changed but I have not.

definition: A sum of money saved or made available for a particular purpose
example: The Malala Fund works to give every girl 12 years of free, safe, quality education.

definition: Give someone the authority or power to do something; make stronger and more confident
example: We must empower girls through education.

definition: Public support for or recommendation of a particular cause or policy
example: Her advocacy brought global attention to the education of girls.

definition: Make forceful or violent efforts to get free of restraint or constriction
example: The struggle for education continues in many parts of the world.

definition: An act of giving up something valued for the sake of something else regarded as more important
example: Let us make our future now, and let us make our dreams tomorrow's reality. Education is worth any sacrifice.

definition: A girl or woman in relation to her parents
example: I tell my story not because it is unique, but because it is the story of many girls. I am those 66 million girls who are deprived of education. And today I am not raising my voice, it is the voice of those 66 million girls.
